---
title: "Global HIV Trends Dashboard"
subtitle: "Comparing HIV incidence and prevalence trends over time in Nigeria, the United States, and India."
format: 
    dashboard:
        orientation: rows
jupyter: python3
execute: 
    echo: false
dashboard: 
    expand: false
theme: cosmo
---


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import country_converter as coco
import plotly.express as px
import itables


# -----------------------------
# 1. Load data
# -----------------------------
total_hiv_raw = pd.read_csv("data/hiv_prevalence.csv")
new_hiv_raw = pd.read_csv("data/hiv_incidence.csv")
population = pd.read_csv("data/population_estimates_world_bank.csv")

# -----------------------------
# 2. Pivot HIV datasets to long format
# -----------------------------
new_hiv_long = new_hiv_raw.melt(
    id_vars=["country", "country_code"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="new_cases"
)

total_hiv_long = total_hiv_raw.melt(
    id_vars=["country", "country_code"],
    var_name="year",
    value_name="total_cases"
)

# Make sure year is numeric
new_hiv_long["year"] = new_hiv_long["year"].astype(int)
total_hiv_long["year"] = total_hiv_long["year"].astype(int)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Join HIV datasets
# -----------------------------
hiv_data_combined = pd.merge(
    new_hiv_long,
    total_hiv_long,
    on=["country", "country_code", "year"],
    how="outer"
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Prepare population data
# -----------------------------
population_long = (
    population
    .query("indicator == 'SP.POP.TOTL'")
    .melt(
        id_vars=["country", "indicator"],
        var_name="year",
        value_name="population"
    )
    .rename(columns={"country": "country_code"})
    .drop(columns=["indicator"])
)

population_long["year"] = population_long["year"].astype(int)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Join population with HIV data
# -----------------------------
hiv_data_combined_pop = pd.merge(
    hiv_data_combined,
    population_long,
    on=["country_code", "year"],
    how="left"
)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Calculate incidence and prevalence rates
# -----------------------------
hiv_data_combined_pop["incidence_rate"] = (
    hiv_data_combined_pop["new_cases"] / hiv_data_combined_pop["population"]
) * 100000

hiv_data_combined_pop["prevalence_rate"] = (
    hiv_data_combined_pop["total_cases"] / hiv_data_combined_pop["population"]
) * 100000

# -----------------------------
# 7. Select countries for line charts
# -----------------------------
countries = ["Nigeria", "United States", "India"]

hiv_data_filtered = (
    hiv_data_combined_pop[hiv_data_combined_pop["country"].isin(countries)]
    .copy()
)

latest_year = hiv_data_filtered["year"].max()

latest_filtered = (
    hiv_data_filtered[hiv_data_filtered["year"] == latest_year]
    .copy()
)

hiv_data_mini = hiv_data_filtered.sort_values("year")

# -----------------------------
# 8. Incidence rate plot
# -----------------------------
fig1 = px.line(
    hiv_data_mini,
    x="year",
    y="incidence_rate",
    color="country",
    title=f"HIV Incidence Rate over Time in {countries}",
    markers=True,
    labels={"incidence_rate": "Incidence Rate (per 100,000)", "year": "Year"}
)

# -----------------------------
# 9. Prevalence rate plot
# -----------------------------
fig2 = px.line(
    hiv_data_mini,
    x="year",
    y="prevalence_rate",
    color="country",
    title=f"HIV Prevalence Rate over Time in {countries}",
    markers=True,
    labels={"prevalence_rate": "Prevalence Rate (per 100,000)", "year": "Year"}
)

# -----------------------------
# 10. Add continent information
# -----------------------------
hiv_data_combined_pop["continent"] = coco.convert(
    names=hiv_data_combined_pop["country"],
    to="continent"
)

# -----------------------------
# 11. Find highest prevalence rate per continent in 2011
# -----------------------------
hiv_2011 = hiv_data_combined_pop[hiv_data_combined_pop["year"] == 2011].copy()

# Remove rows with missing continent or prevalence_rate
hiv_2011 = hiv_2011.dropna(subset=["continent", "prevalence_rate"])

highest_prev_2011 = hiv_2011.loc[
    hiv_2011.groupby("continent")["prevalence_rate"].idxmax()
].sort_values("prevalence_rate", ascending=False)

# -----------------------------
# 12. Bar chart for highest prevalence by continent
# -----------------------------
fig3 = px.bar(
    highest_prev_2011,
    x="continent",
    y="prevalence_rate",
    color="continent",
    hover_data=["country"],
    title="Highest HIV Prevalence Rate per Continent (2011)",
    labels={
        "prevalence_rate": "Prevalence Rate (per 100,000)",
        "continent": "Continent"
    }
)

table = highest_prev_2011[['continent','country','prevalence_rate']]\
    .sort_values('prevalence_rate', ascending=False)\
        .reset_index(drop=True)

```

# HOME

## Row {height="30%"}

::: {.valuebox icon="virus" color="danger" title="HIV New Cases (Selected Countries)"}
`{python} f"{latest_filtered['new_cases'].sum():,.0f}"`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="activity" color="primary" title="Avg Incidence Rate (Selected Countries)"}
`{python} f"{latest_filtered['incidence_rate'].mean():.2f}"`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="heart-pulse" color="success" title="Avg Prevalence Rate (Selected Countries)"}
`{python} f"{latest_filtered['prevalence_rate'].mean():.2f}"`
:::

## ROW {height="70%"}

```{python}
fig1
```

## ROW {height="70%"}

```{python}
fig2
```

# INSIGHTS

## Row

### Highest Prevalence by Continent (2011)
```{python}
fig3
```

### Data for highest prevalence by continent in 2011

```{python}
table
```

# DATA SOURCE

```{python}
itables.show(
    hiv_data_combined_pop,
    caption="Full HIV Data with Population",
    buttons=["csvHtml5"]
)
```


# ABOUT

This dashboard analyzes global HIV data to understand trends in **new infections**, **total cases**, and **prevalence rates** across selected countries ("Nigeria", "United States", "India") over time.

Key insights include:

**HIV new cases** for selected countries in the most recent year available.

**Average incidence rate**, showing how many new HIV infections occur per 100,000 people.

**Average prevalence rate**, indicating how widespread HIV is within the population.

**Trends over time** in incidence and prevalence for Nigeria, the United States, and India.

**Global comparison**, highlighting countries with the highest HIV prevalence rates by continent in 2011.

The data combines **HIV case estimates from Gapminder** with **population data from the World Bank**, allowing infection rates to be standardized per 100,000 people.
